aiashley — global digest Generated 08:54 UTC

Monday, July 13, 2026

International News · 16 Sources · 4 Regions

Daily Brief

The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran has collapsed into renewed hostilities, with Iran's Revolutionary Guards claiming strikes against Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait and Oman in retaliation for US attacks on Iranian territory [France 24, Financial Times, Globe and Mail]. The escalation threatens the 60-day interim agreement meant to establish a framework for permanent peace talks, with both sides disputing control of the Strait of Hormuz. In Paris, Ukraine's Western allies convened a 'Coalition of the Willing' meeting with at least 25 leaders seeking to secure additional air-defence commitments for Kyiv following a NATO summit aimed at demonstrating transatlantic unity [France 24, Le Monde]. US Republican Senator Lindsey Graham died at age 71 from an aortic rupture, creating uncertainty in the narrowly-divided Senate where his absence further reduces the GOP's already precarious majority [BBC, Globe and Mail, RNZ]. A devastating fire at a Bangkok pub killed at least 27 people, with survivors describing how rapidly spreading smoke trapped patrons who fled toward bathrooms with no fire escapes [BBC, Globe and Mail, Le Monde]. Meanwhile, extreme heat continues to strain Europe, with a major wildfire burning through 800 hectares of Fontainebleau forest near Paris, prompting the unprecedented deployment of firefighting aircraft from southern France [BBC, RFI].

Top Stories
01 Conflict

US-Iran Ceasefire Collapses as Tehran Strikes Gulf States in Retaliation for American Attacks

  • Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed strikes against Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait and Oman in response to US airstrikes on Iranian territory, with Kuwait confirming its air defenses were firing Monday morning
  • The US military's Central Command reported hitting dozens of sites including air defence systems, radar installations, missile and drone equipment, with at least one person killed and seven injured in Isfahan province
  • Both sides are disputing control of the Strait of Hormuz, the critical waterway for global oil trade, with the escalation threatening the 60-day interim agreement framework for peace negotiations
  • The renewed fighting comes as both parties attempt to shape the terms of a memorandum of understanding, with Iran interpreting it as granting them leverage over the strait while the US and international community disagree
Coverage
France 24Emphasizes the threat to the interim agreement and includes EU energy task force assessment of jet fuel supplies Financial TimesHeadline focuses on ceasefire teetering; limited content accessible Globe and MailDetailed coverage of both sides' military actions and diplomatic context Al JazeeraIncludes analysis of internal Iranian politics and blame directed at President Pezeshkian RNZAcademic expert analysis suggesting Iran may have upper hand in test of wills
02 Geopolitics

Ukraine's Western Allies Convene in Paris to Bolster Air Defence Support

  • At least 25 leaders joined Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky for a 'Coalition of the Willing' meeting focused on air and missile defence cooperation
  • The gathering follows a NATO summit aimed at demonstrating transatlantic unity and long-term support for Ukraine
  • Russia launched missile and drone attacks on Ukraine on Saturday, killing eight people and wounding dozens, underscoring Kyiv's vulnerability to ballistic missiles
  • Western allies are also working to develop a common position for potential talks with Russia and security guarantees for any eventual peace deal
Coverage
France 24Emphasizes air-defence shortages leaving Ukraine exposed despite battlefield momentum shifts Le MondeArticle inaccessible but title confirms coverage of Paris rally for Ukraine support
03 Policy

US Senator Lindsey Graham Dies at 71; Preliminary Finding Points to Aortic Rupture

  • The South Carolina Republican died Saturday evening after what his office initially described as a 'brief and sudden illness'; medical examiner found aortic dissection related to hardening of arteries
  • Graham had just returned from Kyiv where he met President Zelensky on Friday and spoke with President Trump hours before his death
  • He served in Congress for over three decades, becoming one of Washington's most influential foreign policy voices and a close Trump ally after initially being a vocal critic
  • His death further reduces the already narrow Republican Senate majority, compounding challenges posed by Senator Mitch McConnell's extended absence
Coverage
BBC WorldComprehensive biographical coverage including his evolution from Trump critic to ally Globe and MailTwo articles: one on cause of death, another analyzing his political transformation and Senate implications RNZBrief factual report on death and career highlights
04 Society

Bangkok Bar Fire Kills at Least 27 as Lack of Fire Escapes Traps Patrons

  • Fire broke out near the stage of Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao pub in Bangkok's Chatuchak district just before midnight Sunday, spreading rapidly and cutting power
  • At least 27 people died and 22 remain in critical condition; most victims were found in a bathroom where they had sought shelter with no emergency exits available
  • Preliminary investigation suggests the fire may have been caused by an electrical short circuit in an air conditioner
  • Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul visited the scene; the incident highlights ongoing failures to enforce fire and electrical safety standards despite previous deadly fires
Coverage
BBC WorldNotes this is not the first such incident and that safety standards are poorly enforced despite official promises Globe and MailDetailed account of firefighter response and survivor accounts Le MondeArticle inaccessible but confirms coverage
05 Climate

Major Wildfire Burns Through 800 Hectares of Fontainebleau Forest Near Paris

  • Fire in the historic royal hunting forest about 60 kilometers southeast of Paris had burned through 800 hectares by Monday morning and was still spreading
  • Nearly 400 firefighters and two water bombing planes deployed; officials described the blaze as 'very virulent' and of 'exceptional scale'
  • First time firefighting aircraft have been sent from southern France to tackle fires in the Paris region, reflecting the severity of the third heatwave this year
  • The A6 highway, France's main north-south artery, was partially closed, causing significant travel disruption at the start of the Bastille Day holiday weekend
Coverage
BBC WorldEmphasizes unprecedented nature of deploying southern firefighting aircraft to Paris region RFI EnglishDetails on evacuations, highway closures and firefighting coordination
Also Today
  • South Korean court sentences former President Yoon Suk Yeol to two years for receiving illegal polling services France 24
  • Actor Sam Neill, star of Jurassic Park and The Piano, dies at 78 in Sydney France 24, ABC Australia, Al Jazeera
  • EU expert committee to deliver recommendations on regulating children's access to social media, including potential blanket ban RFI English
  • US Senator Mitch McConnell reveals extended absence was due to fall and mild pneumonia, says he was briefly unconscious BBC World
  • First patients enrolled in record-breaking Ebola treatment trial in DRC, just six weeks after WHO declared public health emergency The Guardian
  • China rebukes New Zealand for supporting South China Sea arbitration ruling on 10th anniversary RNZ
  • At least 13 killed in Spanish wildfire near Bédar, including five believed to be British nationals BBC Europe
  • More than 2,700 deaths in England and Wales linked to record May and June heatwaves, researchers estimate Al Jazeera
  • UK announces £250 million in funding to protect Jewish community following antisemitic attacks Le Monde
  • Jannik Sinner retains Wimbledon title with four-set victory over Alexander Zverev France 24
